transcript: The first step is making sure you're prepared. Make sure that you have a full bag of treats. You can keep them in your pocket, you can get a nifty little treat bag like I have, they have much smaller ones. The appropriate leash is an average six-foot non-bungee leash, not an extender leash. Extender leashes teach dogs to pull. It's the exact opposite of what we're trying to achieve here. Start your walk off with your dog by your side. Nice treat moment. Keep that verbal praise going with occasional food to back it up. One of the most important things to do is reinforce your dog. Good girl. Good girl. She's doing a really great job. I'm seeing totally loose leash here, exactly what I want to see. Good girl! If your dog puts tension on the leash, immediately stop walking. As soon as the tension releases, start walking, praising, and treating. If your dog puts tension on the leash more than twice, do a complete about face. Nope! I'm gonna give him a little tug and walk this way. Okay. Stay strong and your dog will be walking on a loose leash before you know it.
